2603.29944.title: Four Generations of Quantum Biomedical Sensors
2603.29944.paper_summary: Quantum sensing technologies offer transformative potential for ultra-sensitive biomedical sensing, yet their clinical translation remains constrained by classical noise limits and a reliance on macroscopic ensembles. We propose a unifying generational framework to organize the evolving landscape of quantum biosensors based on their utilization of quantum resources. First-generation devices utilize discrete energy levels for signal transduction but follow classical scaling laws. Second-generation sensors exploit quantum coherence to reach the standard quantum limit, while third-generation architectures leverage entanglement and spin squeezing to approach Heisenberg-limited precision. We further define an emerging fourth generation characterized by the end-to-end integration of quantum sensing with quantum learning and variational circuits, enabling adaptive inference directly within the quantum domain. By analyzing critical parameters such as bandwidth matching and sensor-tissue proximity, we identify key technological bottlenecks and propose a roadmap for transitioning from measuring physical observables to extracting structured biological information with quantum-enhanced intelligence.
2603.29944.paper_summary_zh: 量子感測技術為超靈敏生物醫學感測提供了變革潛力，然而它們的臨床轉化仍受到經典噪聲限制和對宏觀集團的依賴所制約。<lb/>我們提出了一個統一的生成框架，以組織基於量子資源利用的量子生物傳感器不斷演變的格局。<lb/>第一代設備利用離散能級進行信號轉導，但遵循經典的縮放法則。<lb/>第二代傳感器利用量子相干性達到標準量子極限，而第三代架構則利用糾纏和自旋擠壓來接近海森堡限制的精度。<lb/>我們進一步定義了一個新興的第四代，特徵是量子感測與量子學習和變分電路的端到端整合，使得在量子領域內直接進行自適應推理成為可能。<lb/>通過分析帶寬匹配和傳感器與組織的接近度等關鍵參數，我們識別出主要的技術瓶頸，並提出了一個從測量物理可觀測量過渡到提取結構化生物信息的量子增強智能的路線圖。
2603.29944.author: Xin Jin et.al.
2603.29944.authors: Xin Jin, Priyam Srivastava, Ronghe Wang, Yuqing Li, Jonathan Beaumariage, Tom Purdy, M. V. Gurudev Dutt, Kang Kim, Kaushik Seshadreesan, Junyu Liu
